deck.author: OpenAI Codex
deck.subject: Browser studio for structured presentation authoring
deck.title: slideotter
deck.objective: Explain how slideotter turns deck editing into a local browser workflow with live structured edits, compact review surfaces, safe apply flows, DOM-first validation, and archived output.
deck.constraints: Keep slide copy concise and describe the active project rather than implementation history.
deck.themeBrief: Clean local-workbench presentation with clear project framing and restrained technical detail.
deck.outline:
1. slideotter
2. Project map
3. The problem is deck drift
4. The studio loop is local
5. Presentations are first-class
6. Context steers the deck
7. Structured specs are the source
8. The preview edits the spec
9. Materials attach to slides
10. One DOM path owns output
11. Candidates stay reviewable
12. Comparison is the decision surface
13. Deck planning works above slides
14. The assistant calls workflows
15. Writes stay narrow
16. Validation reads the rendered deck
17. Output is reproducible
18. The project favors maintainable code
19. Next work stays pragmatic
20. What this enables
